events.title: Loss of Adulis
events.description: The port of Adulis falls to Islamic forces, isolating Aksum and ending its maritime trade.

events.title: Rise of the Zagwe Dynasty
events.description: The political center moves south as Agaw rulers establish power in Lasta.

events.title: Construction of Lalibela Churches
events.description: King Lalibela builds eleven rock-hewn churches to serve as a 'New Jerusalem'.

events.title: The Solomonic Restoration
events.description: Yekuno Amlak overthrows the Zagwe, restoring the Solomonic line of kings.

events.title: Founding of Debre Libanos
events.description: Abba Tekle Haymanot founds a major center of monasticism in Shewa.

events.title: Defeat of the Ifat Sultanate
events.description: Amda Seyon's decisive campaign secures borders and expands the empire.

events.title: Compilation of the Kebra Nagast
events.description: The national epic is codified to legitimize the Solomonic Dynasty's rule.

events.title: Council of Florence
events.description: Zara Yaqob sends an Ethiopian delegation to Italy, establishing formal diplomatic ties with Europe.

events.title: Battle of Gomit
events.description: Zara Yaqob secures a major military victory over Muslim forces.

events.title: Arrival of the Portuguese
events.description: Envoys like Pêro da Covilhã reach the Ethiopian court, ending medieval isolation.
